Title: Court dismisses Indira Gandhi's bid to cite IGP for contempt
Article:
IPOH: The High Court today dismissed M. Indira Gandhi's application for leave to initiate committal proceedings against the Inspector-General of Police and several others over an alleged failure to locate her former husband in efforts to recover their daughter.
Judge Datuk Norsharidah Awang made the decision after hearing submissions from both parties, with no order as to costs.
In her judgment, she said the evidence showed that investigations had been initiated and were still ongoing.
"Under these circumstances, the court is unable to conclude that the proposed contemnors have failed or refused to comply with the court's directions, as the investigation ordered has yet to be completed.
"Accordingly, the court finds that the evidence relied upon by the applicant is insufficient to establish a prima facie case to grant leave for committal proceedings," she said.
"The application is therefore dismissed."
Indira had filed the application on Nov 17, 2025, seeking permission to commence committal proceedings, alleging delays in efforts to trace her former husband, K. Pathmanathan, also known as Muhammad Riduan Abdullah.
She claimed information suggested he had accessed government assistance that could assist in locating him.
She had previously filed a similar application in 2020, which was dismissed by the High Court in 2022. The Court of Appeal later upheld the decision in 2024.
During proceedings, federal counsel said police had reviewed information with the Employees Provident Fund and identified employer details linked to Pathmanathan and his wife in Kelantan, with further investigations ongoing.
Indira's lawyer, however, questioned the pace of the investigation and why only limited checks had been conducted.
The investigating officer told the court that the probe was still ongoing and required more time.
The court fixed Nov 27 for police to update the court on the progress of investigations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MA63: 13 matters resolved, 5 under interim resolution, says Mustapha Sakmud
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A total of 13 out of 29 matters under the Malaysia Agreement 1963 (MA63) official negotiation platform have been fully resolved.
Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Sabah and Sarawak Affairs) Datuk Mustapha Sakmud said another five issues had been given partial or interim resolution following decisions made at the MA63 Technical Committee meeting on March 2.
He said four of the interim matters involved the expansion of state public service posts under Article 112 of the Federal Constitution, as well as issues on health, education and the Borneonisation of the federal public service in Sabah and Sarawak.
"The remaining 11 outstanding matters are being closely monitored by the Sabah and Sarawak Affairs Division, acting as secretariat, together with all stakeholders at federal and state levels," he said during question time at the Dewan Rakyat today.
He was responding to Isnaraissah Munirah Majilis (Warisan–Kota Belud), who asked about the latest status of MA63 implementation and the proposal to increase parliamentary seats for Sabah and Sarawak to 35 per cent.
On the parliamentary seat allocation, Mustapha said the matter remained unresolved and under discussion, as any redelineation exercise can only be carried out by the Election Commission after the expiry of an eight-year cycle.
He added that any change would also require constitutional amendments to the composition of the Dewan Rakyat under Article 46, which must secure a two-thirds majority in the House.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Media Prima launches Ruang Rehat initiative to promote mindful breaks
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Media Prima Bhd has launched 'Ruang Rehat', a multi-platform social initiative aimed at combating stress and encouraging Malaysians to embrace mindful breaks as part of their daily lives.
The campaign, which runs from June to October this year, forms a key pillar of the group's environmental, social and governance (ESG) commitment and seeks to position Media Prima as the nation's official 'pause button'.
Anchored by the tagline 'Jaga Minda, Jaga Hati, Sayangi Diri', the initiative leverages Media Prima's integrated media ecosystem across television, radio, digital and out-of-home platforms to promote proactive mental wellness habits.
The campaign directs audiences to RuangRehat.my, a digital hub featuring wellness articles, a mindfulness playlist, visual therapy zones and other curated content designed to encourage users to take a break and prioritise their emotional well-being.
Media Prima Bhd group managing director Datuk Rafiq Razali said the initiative aims to make mindful breaks a normal part of Malaysian life.
"Our goal is to make mindful breaks a part of Malaysian life. By leveraging the reach of our platforms, we are promoting proactive mental health habits rather than merely responding to crises.
"We want to reduce the stigma surrounding rest and emotional regulation, proving that taking a moment to pause is essential for long-term productivity and well-being," he said.
Media Prima has invited Malaysians to explore the 'Ruang Rehat' campaign and utilise the tools available to enhance their emotional resilience.
Learn more about 'Ruang Rehat' at
RuangRehat.my
or scan the QR code below.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Yinson's net profit climbs 4.3pct to RM120mil in Q1
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Yinson Holdings Bhd's net profit climbed 4.3 per cent to RM120 million in the first quarter ended April 30, 2026 from RM115 million, supported by contributions from the Agogo floating production, storage and offloading (FPSO) vessel.
However, revenue fell 14.7 per cent to RM1.05 billion from RM1.23 million a year ago.
The company declared a dividend of two sen per share, payable on Sept 18, 2026.
In a statement, Yinson group executive chairman Lim Han Weng said the steady quarterly performance was  supported by long-term contracted revenues, a fully operational fleet, strengthened financial resilience and a diversified platform across offshore production, renewables and green technologies.
"During the quarter, we established Yinson Production's Shanghai office to support project execution and client engagement, and celebrated the keel-laying of FSO for Block B, which signals the start of construction, and celebrated the naming of FSO PTSC Lac Da Vang.
"Meanwhile, Yinson GreenTech introduced 300 eDeliver 5 electric vans to the market in collaboration with Weststar Maxus and SAIC Maxus, accelerating EV adoption in Malaysia's commercial sector.
"We remain focused on operational excellence, disciplined capital management and delivering sustainable value as we progress through the year," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Matrade, CAIQ Biosecurity to support exporters for China market
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysia External Trade Development Corporation (Matrade) has entered into a collaboration agreement with CAIQ Biosecurity Sdn Bhd to enhance compliance assistance and improve market preparedness for Malaysian exporters seeking to expand into the Chinese market.
The collaboration is implemented under the Malaysia-China Gateway Initiative (MCGi) to help Malaysian businesses better navigate China's import regulations.
It is aimed particularly at micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs), covering requirements related to product testing, documentation, labelling, traceability and other regulatory procedures.
The initiative helps to strengthen exporters' readiness, reduce compliance-related risks and position Malaysian products as compliant, market-ready and competitive in China.
It covers agriculture, aquatic products, food, beverages and health food; cosmetics; healthcare; pet care; commodities; and selected geographical indication products such as Musang King Durian, Sarawak Pepper and other Malaysian origin-based offerings.
"As China remains a high-potential but technically demanding market, many Malaysian exporters in food, agriculture, cosmetics, health-related products and other regulated sectors require early preparation on product documentation, testing, labelling, registration and traceability requirements for market entry," Matrade said.
Its chairman, Datuk Seri Reezal Merican Naina Merican, said the MCGi is intended to help Malaysian exporters overcome both market access and compliance challenges by offering a more structured, practical and comprehensive route into the Chinese market.
"This framework is timely as Matrade expands its China market strategy beyond first-tier cities to high-potential second- and third-tier cities, where demand for premium, trusted, safe and origin-based products continues to grow," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: In beachside city, Lebanese try to shake off Israel-Hizbollah war
Article:
HEAVY equipment operator Bassam Khalil's bulldozers and excavators are in high demand in south Lebanon's Tyre these days, as the seaside city tries to pick up the pieces from the latest Israel-Hizbollah war.
Residents of destroyed buildings are anxious to comb through the rubble in search of their belongings, the 45-year-old told AFP, next to a pile of shattered concrete and mangled metal that was once a multi-storey apartment block, brought down by an Israeli strike last month.
But often, after they start, "they realise there is nothing left to find", he added, sweating in the dust and summer heat.
Heavy Israeli airstrikes targeted Tyre, one of south Lebanon's largest cities, after Hizbollah dragged the country into the Middle East war on March 2 with rocket fire at Israel in support of its backer Iran.
Piles of rubble and streets strewn with debris dot several mainly seaside neighbourhoods.
Using a bulldozer, Khalil cleared rubble blocking the road to a company's warehouse, near buildings scarred by strikes.
According to the Tyre municipality, the attacks killed around 60 people, destroyed 26 buildings and damaged about 1,000 homes in the city.
Tens of thousands of residents fled after Israel's army warned the entire city to evacuate this month ahead of air raids targeting Hizbollah.
Authorities say more than two-thirds of residents have returned since last week's US-Iran deal to end the wider regional war and the lull in fighting in Lebanon that began over the weekend.
Hussein Hassan, 40, fled north with his family during the war but returned this week to reopen his barbershop, even though one of its walls is cracked and its glass facade has been blown away.
Tyre residents "love life and work. We shake off the dust and rise up again like the phoenix," he said proudly, while welcoming customers.
"Even if there are a dozen wars, we'll come out from under the rubble... and get back to work."
While life has begun returning to the city, Hassan and others told AFP they feared the calm would not last.
"From experience, we don't trust this enemy," said Hassan, alluding to previous hostilities with Israel.
But, he added, "We can't just sit around and wait to die."
[[nid:1472495]]
Despite the uncertainty, and with Israeli troops still in Lebanon, including near Tyre, some residents were already patching up their damaged homes and businesses.
Inside a mini-market with a destroyed wall and facade, Ali Samhat, 26, oversaw the clean-up and repairs.
"We all know that Israel loves destruction... but life goes on. A strike or rocket won't deter us," he said, expressing hope that he might soon be able to reopen.
On a nearby street, another Israeli strike last month flattened a multistorey building.
In it was Abbas Ashour's home, which he had spent his life savings buying and carefully furnishing for him and his family.
Ashour, who lives and works in Britain, fought back tears as he searched for any trace of the flat and the belongings and memories it contained.
"I have valuable things here... from my hard work to the scent of my family's home," said Ashour, 59, putting on protective gloves.
He expressed hope that the Lebanese government would be able to assert its authority against what he branded "the criminal state" of Israel.
In spite of the destruction, Tyre is preparing to launch its summer tourist season on the city's famous beach, deputy mayor Alwan Charafeddine said, with plans for new seaside cafes and kiosks to attract visitors.
Near the sparkling blue water of the Mediterranean, Majed Jaffal, 19, from a town nearby, said he had finally returned to his favourite pastime in Tyre – swimming.
"Since we came back... I've been coming here every day," said Jaffal, who was with his family at a beachside restaurant.
"We missed the sea. There's no seaside in Lebanon like the one in Tyre," he added, calling it the city's "beating heart."
The situation was "calmer" since the strikes ended, noted the computer science student, who has been displaced three times.
"I hope it stays under control."
* The writer is from AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police reports lodged against Puad over claims of palace 'hand' in Johor politics
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Several Johor Barisan Nasional members lodged police reports against former Umno supreme council member Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi after he made allegations against the Johor palace and state Umno.
Currently, at least five reports were lodged at the Kulai, Johor Baru (South) and Seri Alam police headquarters.
The reports were lodged by MCA Youth chief Ling Tian Soon, Kulai BN division chief Datuk Mohd Jafni Md Shukor, Umno Youth vice-chief and Umno supreme council member Mohd Hairi Mad Shah, Johor Baru Umno chief Datuk Yahya Jaafar and Johor Umno secretary Datuk Abdul Halim Suleiman.
This follows Puad's announcement of his immediate resignation from Umno during which he claimed Johor Umno no longer free to make its own decisions because of undue influence in the state's political affairs.
He also alleged that the Johor assembly was dissolved on June 1 following a directive from the palace.
Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has
refuted the claims
.
Following the resignation, Umno secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said Puad's discontent stemmed from his
agenda for his son
to become the BN candidate for the Rengit constituency.
Meanwhile, Jafni said the report was not intended to restrict freedom of speech or stifle public debate, but to ensure that the serious allegations are investigated through proper legal channels.
He said the accusations against Onn Hafiz and key state institutions could not be allowed to circulate as mere perception or speculation without verification.
"The report is to ensure these claims are investigated according to the law," he said, adding that the matter should no longer remain in the realm of political rhetoric or social media discourse.
Jafni also indicated that those making allegations must be prepared to substantiate them with facts that can withstand scrutiny.
He urged all parties to respect legal processes and avoid spreading unverified claims that could undermine public trust in state institutions.
He added that politicians should avoid touching on the 3R (race, religion and royalty) issues as they are highly sensitive and can undermine public harmony if mishandled.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hamzah: If Bersatu turns adversarial, Wawasan will respond in kind
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Parti Cinta Malaysia, now known as Parti Wawasan Negara, will assess Bersatu's position and statements before deciding the nature of its cooperation with the party, says its president Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin.
Asked whether Wawasan would continue working with Bersatu within Perikatan Nasional (PN) following his expulsion from the party, Hamzah said he was monitoring developments closely.
"I am observing how they respond to this matter," he said.
"If they want to become adversaries, then adversaries it is," he told reporters at his office today.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Asian stocks rebound as South Korea shares surge on AI optimism; currencies lag
Article:
SINGAPORE: A surge in South Korean stocks helped a gauge tracking Asian equities to regain its footing on Thursday, with upbeat Micron Technology results reviving confidence in AI demand, while regional currencies weakened against a strong US dollar.
The MSCI Emerging Markets Asia gauge was about two per cent higher, rebounding from a near two-week low. South Korea's KOSPI index rose as much as six per cent, on track for its best session since June 12.
Over two sessions, the index, a heavyweight on the MSCI gauge, has risen nearly 8 per cent from Tuesday's 10 per cent drop - the steepest since March - that sparked a global tech selloff.
The volatility underscores investor concerns that valuations for AI-related companies have become stretched following years of gains.
South Korean market regulator's signals that the sector's rally had gotten overheated added to those fears. Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix shares rose by 6.2 per cent and 11.6 per cent, respectively, on Thursday, as chipmakers rallied, tracking Micron Technology's upbeat quarterly results.
SK Hynix's plans to raise up to US$29.4 billion through a US stock market listing in what would be among the biggest listings globally, also lent support to the positive sentiment.
"Targeted sell-off suggests investors are enforcing strict valuation discipline following an extended, aggressive run in AI-adjacent and speculative growth equities ... serving notice that realised earnings must now step up to justify stretched multiples," said Chris Strazzeri, financial dealing manager at Moomoo Australia and New Zealand.
"And that's pretty much what Micron delivered after-market, with its powerhouse result boosting tech sentiment overall." Taiwanese shares, another tech-heavy index in the region, rose as much as 1.6 per cent.
Elsewhere, Thailand's stock index rose over 1 per cent, a day after its central bank kept its rates steady, while upgrading its economic growth forecasts. Indonesian equities rose 1.5 per cent, on course to snap a three-day losing streak, while stocks in Manila traded in the green.
The dollar was heading toward its sharpest monthly gain in almost a year on Thursday, as traders bet a strong US economy would prop up short-term interest rates, putting pressure on the emerging Asian currencies.
"Asia FX has come under broad-based pressure against the US dollar, with several Asean currencies leading losses since last week's FOMC meeting," MUFG analyst Lloyd Chan said in a note. "A high-for-longer US rates environment is likely to remain a near-term headwind for regional currencies, particularly lower-yielding ones." Philippine peso led losses, declining 0.6 per cent.
The currency was set to lose for the seventh time in the last eight sessions.
South Korean won and the Taiwan dollar both inched 0.3 per cent lower, while the Indonesian rupiah traded largely flat. The Thai baht eked out marginal gains, snapping a six-session streak of declines.
"The recent THB weakness came from relatively strong levels amid still-robust external position, which could support tourism and ease financial conditions for smaller exporters," said Chua Han Teng, Senior Economist at DBS.
Malaysian ringgit and the Indian rupee went against the tide to trade higher, each appreciating over 0.5 per cent. In other news, powerful earthquakes struck Venezuela on Wednesday afternoon, toppling buildings in the capital Caracas, trapping people in the rubble and prompting scientists to warn of potentially heavy casualties and widespread destruction.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Indonesia sets 3-month transition period for B50 biodiesel programme, official says
Article:
JAKARTA: Indonesia has issued a regulation to implement its B50 biodiesel mandate starting from July 1, with a three-month transition period for retailers to clear their existing stocks, a senior energy ministry official said on Thursday.
The launch of the B50 programme on July 1 means that all gasoil sold in the country must contain 50 per cent palm-based diesel and 50 per cent conventional diesel.
The programme is a response to the oil supply disruptions that followed the attacks on Iran by the United States and Israel. The currently operating B40 policy stipulates that fuel must contain 40 per cent palm-based diesel.
The new B50 regulation, the details of which have not been made public, includes a three-month transition period to allow fuel retailers to clear out their existing stocks, said the director general of renewable energy, Eniya Listiani Dewi.
Indonesia provides subsidies for its biodiesel mandate, using proceeds from a levy on palm oil exports, to bridge the gap between gasoil and palm-based diesel prices.
"(State energy firm) Pertamina pledged to clear out their stocks within two months … but for smaller fuel retailers we are giving some adjustment: three months," Eniya said.
*Indonesia also plans to mix non-subsidised gasoline with at least five per cent ethanol in Java, the country's most populated island, over the 2026-2027 period, and raise the proportion to 10 per cent by 2028.
On Thursday, Energy Minister Bahlil Lahadalia told an energy forum that the biodiesel mandate "saves Indonesia's face from gasoil imports."
The biodiesel mandate for this year, a mix of B40 in the first six months and B50 from July to December, is estimated to save Indonesia around 157.28 trillion rupiah (US$8.89 billion) in import costs, the energy ministry said, compared to an estimated 139.8 trillion rupiah saved if the B40 scheme was maintained over the whole year.
The ministry has yet to issue an additional biodiesel allocation for this year to meet rising demand from the B50 programme. Under the B40 mandate, the ministry has allocated 15.46 million kilolitres.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Batik Air launches direct Kuala Lumpur-Shanghai service to boost China connectivity
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Batik Air has launched its new direct service between Kuala Lumpur and Shanghai, further strengthening its presence in China.
The carrier said its inaugural flight departed Kuala Lumpur on Tuesday and arrived at Shanghai Pudong International Airport at 1.20 am the following morning, while the return service subsequently arrived at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) Terminal 1 at 8.50am yesterday.
"The introduction of the Shanghai – Kuala Lumpur service comes at an opportune time as Malaysia intensifies efforts under the Visit Malaysia 2026 (VM2026) campaign.
The national tourism initiative targets 43 million tourist arrivals by 2026, with China remaining one of Malaysia's key source markets," it said in a statement.
Batik Air chief executive officer Datuk Chandran Rama Muthy said the new route represents more than just enhanced air connectivity, serving as a catalyst for stronger economic, tourism and cultural exchanges between Malaysia and China.
"Shanghai is one of China's most dynamic and influential cities, and we are delighted to welcome travellers from this important market to Malaysia. The launch of this service strengthens the longstanding relationship between Malaysia and China while creating greater opportunities for tourism, trade and investment between both nations," he said.
Chandran said the route would play an important role in showcasing Malaysia's diverse tourism offerings ahead of VM2026, with travellers also able to connect to destinations across the country through Batik Air's domestic network.
"We remain committed to supporting Malaysia's tourism aspirations while providing seamless and convenient travel options for passengers travelling between China and Malaysia," he said.
Batik Air said the launch of the Shanghai-Kuala Lumpur route reinforces its commitment to expanding regional connectivity and strengthening Malaysia's position as a key aviation gateway in the Asia-Pacific region. — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: UK's Reeves backs Burnham for prime minister, defers on own role
Article:
LONDON: British finance minister Rachel Reeves said on Thursday she was backing Andy Burnham to be the next prime minister, brushing off reports she could lose her role and be moved to a more junior post if he took over the Labour Party.
"I'm supporting Andy to be prime minister," she told the BBC, after Burnham emerged as the only declared candidate to replace Keir Starmer, who announced his resignation on Monday.
Starmer said he would oversee an orderly transition after bowing to pressure over weak poll ratings and poor local election results, triggering a leadership contest set to begin on July 9.
Reeves, a close ally of Starmer, said no one could doubt her commitment to the outgoing prime minister, adding that she had worked alongside him for six years.
Burnham, who is the only candidate to have put his name forward, is widely expected to be installed without a challenge, meaning he could become prime minister by mid-July.
If appointed, he would be Britain's seventh leader in a decade.
Asked about reports she could be demoted, Reeves said cabinet decisions would be a matter for Burnham.
"I'm not going to pre-empt the decisions that the new prime minister will make. I'm backing Andy. I think he'd be a great prime minister, but those are his decisions, not mine," she said.
She said she stood ready to provide targeted, temporary support on energy bills later this year.
Reeves said the next leader should stick to her fiscal rules, including balancing day-to-day spending with tax revenues and reducing debt as a share of output.
Burnham has previously signalled he would keep current borrowing rules in place.
"I know that whoever is prime minister and chancellor in the future will inherit a stronger economy than the one I inherited two years ago," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man found dead in parked car in George Town
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: A 55-year-old man was found dead inside a parked car along Jalan Lintang Merdeka here today.
The condition of the body indicated that the man had likely been dead for more than four days.
Northeast district police chief Assistant Commissioner Abdul Rozak Muhammad said police received a report at 9.50am after members of the public alerted authorities to an unconscious man inside a vehicle.
He said investigations revealed that a cleaning worker had noticed the car parked under suspicious circumstances at 9.30am before contacting police.
"The victim, a man from Bukit Mertajam, was found in the driver's seat of the car, fully clothed, and the vehicle was unlocked.
"Checks conducted on the body and at the scene did not uncover any signs of foul play.
"The deceased also had a history of chronic illness and had been receiving treatment at a hospital," he said in a statement today.
Abdul Rozak said the condition of the body indicated that the man had likely died more than four days before he was discovered.
He said the body was sent to the Forensic Department of Penang Hospital for a post-mortem examination.
Police have classified the case as a sudden death report pending the outcome of further investigations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kelantan Umno hits out at Puad Zarkashi after resignation
Article:
KOTA BARU: The decision by Umno Supreme Council member Datuk Dr Puad Zarkashi to quit the party reflects dissatisfaction and political immaturity.
Kelantan Umno information chief Datuk Zawawi Othman said Puad's allegation that Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi was acting under instructions was baseless and failed to recognise the close working relationship between Umno and the royal institution in state administration.
"The accusation is unfounded. Instead, it demonstrates the strong cooperation between Umno and the Johor palace in managing the affairs of the state for the people.
"Umno has always upheld and defended the institution of the Malay Rulers.
"In fact, it is the only political party whose constitution explicitly protects the position of the Malay rulers," he said when contacted today.
Commenting on Puad's criticism over the timing of the dissolution of the Johor State Legislative Assembly, Zawawi described the matter as minor, noting that the state administration had exceeded four years in office and was due for an election.
"Whether the dissolution takes place a week, a month earlier or later is not a major issue. It merely reflects respect and loyalty to the ruler.
"This is not something that should lead to a public dispute or be used to undermine the party," he said.
Zawawi also urged party leaders and supporters not to allow personal frustrations to be used as political ammunition by opponents.
"We should not allow our own foolishness or childish behaviour to be exploited by others.
"Surely we do not want our leaders and supporters to be seen acting irrationally over minor issues involving the royal institution," he added.
Puad announced his
immediate resignation
from Umno today, saying Johor Umno was no longer free to make its own decisions.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: 15 Bersama candidates to declare assets through statutory declaration
Article:
JOHOR BARU: All 15 Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) candidates contesting in the upcoming Johor election will declare their assets, income and expenditure through statutory declarations.
In a statement today, Bersama said the candidates would also sign four statutory undertakings and a conditional letter of resignation, including agreement to a RM2 million penalty bond should they breach the anti-party-hopping pledge.
"Details of assets, debts, income and expenditure for each candidate will be uploaded to the Bersama website for public access from 10pm tomorrow," the statement said.
The party added that it would also submit its overall campaign expenditure statement and funding sources after the end of the campaigning period.
Meanwhile, Bersama said its candidate announcement ceremony would be held at 8pm tomorrow at the Paragon Market Place car park here.
The Election Commission has set July 11 as polling day for the Johor state election, with nominations on June 27 and early voting on July 7.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Wawasan to skip Johor polls, contest Negri Sembilan election
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Parti Cinta Malaysia, now known as Parti Wawasan Negara, will not contest the Johor state election but will support Perikatan Nasional, says its newly appointed president Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin.
The Larut MP said, however, that the party would contest the Negri Sembilan election, adding that its participation had been confirmed following his election as party president at its annual general meeting on June 20.
"For now, we have decided not to contest the Johor election. Not contesting does not mean we are not supporting.
"We will contest Negri Sembilan. I want to make it clear here that it is confirmed," he told reporters at his office today.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mindef launches new defence strategy amid evolving global threats
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Defence Ministry (Mindef) has developed two strategic plans to respond to evolving global threats.
Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the two documents, the National Defence Strategic Plan (NDSP) and the Defence Capacity Blueprint (DCB), will guide Malaysia's defence priorities from 2026 to 2030.
He said this was crucial as armed conflicts, including those in the Middle East and Ukraine, continue to affect global stability.
He said the mid-term review of the Defence White Paper identified gaps and led to the development of the NDSP, which is anchored on seven strategic pillars.
"Which are capability empowerment and army readiness, strengthened defence capacity, workforce well-being, civil-military cooperation for total defence, science, technology and industry development.
"It also includes international defence relations, and veteran welfare," he said at the National Defence Strategic Launch 2026–2030 and Government Capacity Action Framework here today.
Khaled said the plans will improve performance monitoring and accountability through integrated systems, regular reviews and data-driven platforms to measure outcomes and impact.
"The plans will ensure the financial, human, leadership, technological and institutional capacities that will deliver orderly capacity management across the defence ecosystem."
Khaled said Malaysia will continue active international engagement through Asean defence forums, bilateral exercises and global dialogues, reflecting the view that modern defence must be collective.
He added that rebranding the strategy as the NDSP marks a shift, with defence now a national agenda involving all Malaysians to strengthen resilience against diverse threats.
"Operational milestones include delivery of three Medium-Altitude Long-Endurance Unmanned Aerial Systems in March 2026, now operational at Labuan Air Base, with upcoming acquisitions such as Fighter Lead-In Trainer Light Combat Aircraft, Maritime Patrol Aircraft and Littoral Mission Ship Batch 2.
"Future capability development will emphasise AI integration, cyber defence, electronic warfare, electromagnetic spectrum mastery and defence satellite capabilities led by the Defence Cyber and Electromagnetic Command."
Khaled said the plans also commit to personnel welfare, including six new armed forces family housing projects, maintenance of 20,000 units, two new polyclinics, hospital upgrades and economic support programmes, including army Madani initiatives.
Khaled said the plans aim to build a modern and ready force, while ensuring every ringgit strengthens national readiness and the welfare of the army.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PRG shareholders reject three key AGM resolutions
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Shareholders of PRG Holdings Bhd voted against three key resolutions at the company's annual general meeting (AGM) today.
They have voted against proposals to approve directors' fees and allowances, grant the board authority to issue new shares and undertake a share buy-back exercise.
Datuk Sheah Kok Fah, PRG's second-largest shareholder with an 8.14 per cent stake, said the outcome sends a strong message that shareholders are concerned about the company's governance, transparency and accountability standards.
He said the rejection of the directors' remuneration proposal indicates that shareholders are unwilling to approve payments to the board without first seeing meaningful corrective action on unresolved issues, including related-party transactions, impairments and losses suffered by the company.
Shareholders also declined to give the board a fresh mandate to issue shares, reflecting concerns over governance matters and the potential dilution of existing shareholders' interests.
The proposed share buy-back mandate was similarly rejected, suggesting that investors are reluctant to grant the board additional capital management powers while outstanding issues within the company remain unresolved.
Meanwhile, shareholders approved the re-election of independent non-executive directors Tan Sri Dr Mazlan Lazim and Datin Arlina Ariff, as well as the reappointment of BDO PLT as auditors.
According to Sheah, the re-election of the two directors ensures the board remains at six members, providing greater balance and oversight rather than reducing its size and concentrating decision-making power.
He added that retaining BDO PLT would provide continuity in the audit process at a time when significant new information has emerged regarding the company's affairs.
On the AGM outcome, Sheah said shareholders had clearly expressed concerns over governance and disclosure practices within PRG.
"The AGM is only the first step. Subject to shareholder support, we intend to requisition an extraordinary general meeting to seek changes that we believe are necessary to improve governance and accountability within the company.
"These proposals may include the removal of the current group managing director and the appointment of new directors for the protection of all shareholders.
"My objective is not disruption, but to strengthen the company, protect minority shareholders and restore market confidence in PRG," he said.
The governance concerns centre on transactions involving PRG subsidiary Premier Construction (International) Sdn Bhd (PCI) and Premier De Muara Sdn Bhd (PDM).
Questions first arose after PRG announced a proposed debt settlement involving RM37.17 million owed by PDM to PCI.
The arrangement involved the transfer of 12 condominium units valued at RM13.73 million, leaving a remaining balance of RM23.44 million without a clearly disclosed recovery mechanism.
PRG later terminated the agreement, citing related-party transaction concerns and PDM's failure to disclose a court judgement affecting its obligations.
Subsequently, the company disclosed that PCI had issued a statutory demand to PDM for RM64.24 million in outstanding payments, significantly higher than the amount previously disclosed.
The sharp increase has raised questions among shareholders over the company's actual financial exposure and the adequacy of disclosures made to the market.
Sheah said shareholders deserve clarity on the true financial position of the company, who benefited from the transactions, and who ultimately bears the financial consequences.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man suffers burns, 3 injured in LPG fire at Pasir Gudang flat
Article:
PASIR GUDANG: A man suffered burns and three family members were injured in a fire believed to have been caused by a leaking LPG hose at their Taman Air Biru home last night.
A Johor Fire and Rescue Department spokesperson said the 30-year-old man sustained burns to 20 per cent of his legs and 50 per cent of his hands in the blaze at the kitchen of a third-floor unit at Block 14, SEDC Flats.
The department received an emergency call at 9.26pm, with 10 firefighters, a Fire and Rescue Tender unit and an Emergency Medical Rescue Services team from the Pasir Gudang station arriving at 9.31pm.
"The distance to the location was about 4km," he said.
The operation was led by Senior Fire Officer 1 Faisal Ismail.
"The fire is believed to have been triggered by a leakage of LPG gas from the hose," he said.
"We were informed that the family had earlier detected a smell of gas before the incident."
He said the 20ft by 40ft unit was about 50 per cent destroyed in the fire.
Three family members were injured in the incident – the man, his 28-year-old wife who sustained injuries and bleeding on her forehead, and their four-year-old daughter who suffered burns on her right big toe.
"All three managed to escape before being rushed to Pasir Gudang Hospital by ambulance from the Taman Air Biru Health Clinic," he said.
The fire was brought under control at 10.32pm and fully extinguished at 10.40pm.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 11 developers registered with CRESS as of May 2026
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A total of 11 developers have registered under the Corporate Renewable Energy Supply Scheme (CRESS) as of May 2026.
The number includes eight green consumers from the data centre, high-technology and manufacturing sectors, involving a combined renewable energy (RE) capacity of about three gigawatts, said Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Energy Transition and Water Transformation Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof.
"Their participation reflects positive interest from industry players in the direct green electricity supply mechanism, particularly to meet the needs of large-scale corporate consumers," he said during the Minister's Question Time in the Dewan Rakyat today.
Fadillah said this in response to a supplementary question from Rodziah Ismail (PH-Ampang) on CRESS's status of implementation and the number of participants in the scheme.
He said CRESS was launched on July 26, 2024, as a mechanism that enables corporate consumers to procure green electricity directly from RE developers through access to the national grid network.
He said the implementation of CRESS also supports the country's aspiration to accelerate RE development in line with the target of achieving 70 per cent RE share of installed capacity by 2050 under the National Energy Transition Roadmap (NETR).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man arrested hours after mosque handbag robbery in Penang
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: A 54-year-old man was arrested hours after allegedly robbing a woman of her handbag during dawn prayers at a mosque here yesterday.
Northeast district police chief Assistant Commissioner Abdul Rozak Muhammad said police received a report at 8.41am yesterday from a 48-year-old woman claiming her handbag was snatched by an unknown man at a mosque along Jalan Kapitan Keling.
He said investigations showed the incident occurred at 6.15am while the victim was performing Subuh prayers in the mosque's women's prayer area.
"The woman reportedly noticed the suspect attempting to take her handbag and tried to stop him, resulting in a struggle. However, the suspect managed to escape with the handbag.
"The victim suffered losses estimated at RM5,000.
"Following swift investigations, police arrested a 54-year-old man from Batu Caves, Selangor, at 5.27pm the same day at the Raja Tun Uda Ferry Terminal.
"The suspect was believed to have been attempting to flee using the ferry service when he was detained," he said in a statement.
Abdul Rozak said police also recovered the victim's belongings from the suspect during the arrest.
The case is being investigated under Section 392 of the Penal Code for robbery.
If convicted, the offender faces up to 14 years' imprisonment, as well as a fine or whipping.
The suspect has been remanded for seven days from today until July 1 following an order by the George Town magistrate's court to assist investigations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BN new faces vow strong push for Johor victory in state election
Article:
JOHOR BARU: New faces from Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) component parties are determined to deliver victory for the coalition in the 16th state election on July 11.
The first-time candidates from Umno, MCA and MIC are committed to securing a strong mandate for BN, including a two-thirds majority to form the state government.
BN candidate for Bukit Kepong, Ahmad Syar'e Yusof, 51, said although this marked his first time contesting, he was confident of reclaiming the seat for the coalition.
"Even though this seat is currently held by the opposition, I have been on the ground serving the local community over the past few years.
"As a new candidate, I want to ensure continuity of plans under the previous state administration, including the Maju Johor 2030 agenda," he said after the BN candidate announcement ceremony at the Johor Umno liaison office yesterday.
MCA candidate for Puteri Wangsa, Teow Chia Ling, said she was prepared for a tough contest, including against Pakatan Harapan's Dr Maszlee Malik.
"Although this is my first time contesting, I am not new to Puteri Wangsa as I have been engaging with residents here for years.
"I will meet as many voters as possible during the campaign to understand their issues and aspirations," she said.
Kahang candidate V. Rugendran, 29, the youngest BN candidate in the election, said age would not be a barrier in serving the people.
"What matters is the ability to carry out responsibilities effectively," he said, adding that he would also use social media to reach younger voters.
Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi yesterday announced all
56 BN candidates
contesting in the state election on July 11.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police report lodged over Puad's claims linking Johor palace to state assembly dissolution
Article:
JOHOR BARU: A police report has been lodged over statements by former Umno Supreme Council member
Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi
, who allegedly linked the Johor palace to the dissolution of the state assembly and claimed that Johor Umno was under royal influence.
MCA Youth chief Ling Tian Soon lodged the report after Puad allegedly claimed that caretaker Johor menteri besar
Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi
had told him the state assembly was dissolved on the palace's "instructions."
Ling also said Puad had alleged that Johor Umno was being controlled by the palace.
"I have lodged a police report over Puad's statements, which alleged that Onn Hafiz had informed him that the state assembly was dissolved on the instructions from the palace and that Johor Umno is controlled by the royal institution," he said in a Facebook post.
He said the allegations were untrue and had already been publicly denied by Onn Hafiz.
Ling said the state assembly was dissolved strictly in accordance with Article 23, Part II of the Johor State Constitution 1895, after the menteri besar presented the matter to Tunku Mahkota Ismail Sultan Ibrahim, Regent of Johor, and obtained royal assent.
He said royal assent was a constitutional requirement under the state constitution and should not be misconstrued as political instruction or interference.
"As such, Puad's statements, which appear to suggest that the palace directed the dissolution or interfered in political affairs, are serious, misleading and capable of creating negative perceptions of Johor's constitutional monarchy," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Chegubard pleads not guilty to sedition over Negri royal institution remarks
Article:
SEREMBAN: Political activist Badrul Hisham Shaharin, better known as Chegubard, pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to publishing seditious content on Facebook allegedly directed at the Negri Sembilan Royal Institution.
The 48-year-old entered the plea before Judge Datin Surita Budin after the charge was read out to him.
According to the charge, Badrul Hisham allegedly published the content on Facebook under the account "Che GuBard" about 8.30pm on May 26 in Kuala Pilah.
He was charged under Section 4(1) of the Sedition Act 1948, which carries a maximum fine of RM5,000, imprisonment of up to three years, or both upon conviction.
Deputy public prosecutor Datuk Ahmad Sazali Omar proposed bail of RM10,000 and applied for a gag order barring the accused from making statements relating to the Negri Sembilan Royal Institution.
Defence counsel Muhammad Rafique Rashid Ali sought lower bail of RM2,500 and objected to the application.
The court set bail at RM5,000 with one surety and ordered Badrul Hisham to report to the nearest police station once a month.
The court also dismissed the application for a gag order and fixed Aug 3 for case management.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia to roll out water reclamation policy to support industrial demand, data centres
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is drafting a National Water Reclamation Policy to expand the use of recycled water for industrial activities, including data centres, while preserving treated water supplies for households.
Deputy Prime Minister and Energy Transition and Water Transformation Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof said the policy would be supported by new implementation strategies and extensive stakeholder engagement.
"We are planning to set it up and will come up with new policies and strategies for the way forward," he said after officiating the 5th World Digital Economy and Technology Summit (WDET) 2026 today.
"We have a lot of water. The issue is separating water for consumption from water for industry," he said.
Malaysia currently produces 48.5 million litres of reclaimed water daily and aims to raise output to 118 million litres per day by 2030.
He said treated potable water would continue to be prioritised for public consumption, while reclaimed water processed from wastewater would be supplied to industrial users.
"This reclaimed water is meant solely for industrial use, not for drinking or domestic consumption," said Fadillah.
He added Johor has already adopted the model through a collaboration between Indah Water Konsortium (IWK) and Air Johor, with similar initiatives being developed in Selangor and Negeri Sembilan.
Separately, he said a special task force chaired by the Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry (Miti) has begun evaluating data centre investments based on their energy and water requirements.
According to Fadillah, the task force comprises relevant ministries and agencies, with similar assessment mechanisms being rolled out at the state level before major industrial investments are approved.
"We want to ensure that any investment coming into Malaysia uses the latest technology that the country truly needs.
"It should not simply be another data centre, but one that generates economic returns, contributes to societal well-being, and helps develop our workforce so they are equipped with new technologies," he said.
The government will also engage federal ministries, state governments, industry players and non-governmental organisations before finalising the policy framework.
"The task force will ensure that every data centre investment aligns with the availability of reclaimed water and energy supply.
"We will only approve projects if we have sufficient capacity in terms of both energy and water resources. This framework will also be implemented at the state level," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: New Pahang police HQ project back on track after land issues resolved
Article:
KUANTAN: Construction of the new Pahang police contingent headquarters in Sultan Ahmad Shah Administrative Centre (KotaSAS) here will resume after issues involving the project site were resolved.
Bukit Aman Logistics and Technology Department (Technical/Operations) director Commissioner Redzuan Abd Hamid said the project had previously received budget approval but was delayed due to land-related matters.
"Now that the land issues have been resolved, the project can proceed as planned. Since the budget has already been allocated, we will ensure it moves forward.
"I am committed to seeing the project progress before I retire next year," he told reporters after witnessing the handover ceremony of the Pahang police chief today.
Outgoing Pahang police chief Datuk Seri Yahaya Othman, who retired today, is succeeded by his deputy, Datuk Azry Akmar Ayob, in an acting capacity.
Redzuan said the new headquarters is expected to cost about RM300 million.
He expressed hope that Azry would continue pushing for the project's implementation.
While no completion date has been set, Redzuan assured that construction would proceed as planned.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim announced in
Budget 2025
an allocation for the new Pahang police headquarters, alongside a district police headquarters in Petaling Jaya and several police stations nationwide.
The existing Pahang police headquarters on Jalan Gambut, built in 1984, has become increasingly congested due to its location in the city centre.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Indonesia weighs US$2 billion cut to Prabowo's signature free meals programme
Article:
JAKARTA: Indonesia is preparing to scale back President Prabowo Subianto's flagship free meals programme, with officials weighing another budget cut of more than US$2 billion along with reductions in kitchens and beneficiaries, according to two sources familiar with the matter and an internal presentation.
The planned move marks one of the clearest indications yet of mounting fiscal and governance pressures on Prabowo's most important and expensive campaign pledge, which has been central to concerns about Indonesia's fiscal discipline.
Two sources familiar with the plans told Reuters the National Nutrition Agency (NNA), which runs the programme, is targeting a reduction of at least 15 per cent from its 268 trillion rupiah (US$16 billion) budget this year - equivalent to about 40 trillion rupiah - after identifying inefficiencies.
One of them said the cut could be 50 trillion rupiah.
The two sources said the exact reduction was not known. But a third said an internal review could cut recipients to 49 million from 62.5 million, though the assessment is ongoing and subject to change.
The sources spoke on condition of anonymity as the matter was still private.
A spokesperson for the Ministry of Finance told Reuters in a written reply on Thursday that it is "awaiting a budget sharpening plan" for the programme from the NNA, and will coordinate on any implementation.
The NNA and the president's office did not immediately respond to Reuters' requests for comment.
The details of the rollback plan, including the cut in recipients and internal discussions, have not been reported before.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Haim Hilman charged with making statements likely to cause public alarm
Article:
SEREMBAN: Jitra assemblyman Dr Haim Hilman Abdullah pleaded not guilty at the magistrate's court here today to a charge of making statements likely to cause public alarm.
The Kedah executive councillor for Industry and Investment, Higher Education, Science, Technology and Innovation entered the plea before magistrate Nurul Saqinah Rosli.
According to the charge, Haim Hilman, 53, allegedly made statements with intent to cause public alarm or disturb public order between 8pm and midnight on June 13 at the Pas Seremban headquarters along Jalan Jelebu.
He was charged under Section 505(b) of the Penal Code, which carries a maximum sentence of two years' imprisonment, a fine, or both upon conviction.
State prosecution director Ku Hayati Ku Haron sought bail of RM10,000 and requested that the accused surrender his passport.
Defence lawyer Mohd Fauzi Che Abu, however, applied for lower bail, saying his client frequently travelled overseas on official duties as a senior state government leader.
The court allowed bail at RM5,000 with one surety and fixed July 30 for mention.
On June 15, the Higher Education Ministry took a serious view of allegations made by
Haim Hilman
regarding the public university admissions system, describing them as unfounded.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia does not recognise Myanmar administration, says Tok Mat
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia has never recognised Myanmar's post-election administration, and recent engagement with officials in Naypyidaw should not be construed as diplomatic recognition.
Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said his visit to Myanmar in May was part of Asean's efforts to maintain communication channels and advance peace initiatives under the Five-Point Consensus (5PC), not an endorsement of the country's leadership.
"I have already stated that we do not recognise them. When we do not recognise them, the term 'government' does not apply from Malaysia's perspective," he told the Dewan Rakyat today.
He was responding to William Leong Jee Keen (PH-Selayang), who asked about Malaysia's current policy towards Myanmar and the role of the 5PC in shaping that position.
Mohamad said Malaysia's stance remained unchanged despite his meeting with Myanmar Foreign Minister U Than Swe during a two-day visit to Naypyidaw from May 19 to 20.
"The visit was not a recognition of the new Myanmar administration after the election and does not in any way affect Malaysia's and Asean's position on the implementation of the Five-Point Consensus," he said.
He said the visit followed a decision by Asean leaders at the 48th Asean Summit in Cebu on May 8, which called for informal engagement with Myanmar to help break the deadlock in resolving the country's prolonged political crisis.
Mohamad said discussions focused on political stability, progress in implementing the 5PC, the possibility of inclusive dialogue involving all stakeholders, national reconciliation efforts and developments on the ground.
He also raised concerns over political detainees, the Rohingya refugee issue and cooperation in combating online financial crimes.
Mohamad added that Malaysia had suggested Myanmar consider greater autonomy arrangements and elements of federalism as part of a long-term political solution.
He said Asean could not afford to isolate Myanmar completely, adding that doing so could create a vacuum that external powers may seek to exploit.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bankrupt man claims trial over acting as company director
Article:
BUTTERWORTH: A bankrupt man has claimed trial at the Sessions Court here to a charge of acting as a company director without court approval.
In a statement today, the Companies Commission of Malaysia (SSM) reminded the public that enforcement action would be taken against individuals found breaching provisions under laws regulated by the commission.
Yusri Husain pleaded not guilty before Judge Roslan Hamid on June 18 to an offence under Section 198(1)(a) of the Companies Act 2016.
According to the charge sheet, Yusri acted as a director of Elit Mestika Global (M) Sdn Bhd on Nov 18, 2024.
At the time, he was an undischarged bankrupt and had not obtained court approval to act as a company director.
If convicted, he faces imprisonment of up to five years, a fine of up to RM1 million, or both under Section 198(7) of the Act.
Roslan allowed bail at RM3,000 with one surety.
The court fixed July 22 for case mention and submission of documents by the prosecution.
The prosecution was conducted by SSM prosecuting officer Amir Sharifuddin Hashim, while Yusri was unrepresented.
SSM said it remained committed to ensuring compliance with laws under its administration.
"We are committed to ensuring compliance with laws under our administration.
"We will continue promoting corporate responsibility and encourage the business community to uphold integrity and good governance practices," it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Be more proactive’: Judge reprimands police over delay in locating Indira Gandhi’s ex-husband
Article:
IPOH, June 25 — The High Court has granted police an additional six months to track down Muhammad Riduan Abdullah, the ex-husband of M. Indira Gandhi.
Judge Datuk Norsharidah Awang has fixed November 27 for the next judicial monitoring session to review the progress of the arrest warrant against Riduan.
While acknowledging the heavy workload and challenges facing the force, Norsharidah warned that any new leads regarding Riduan’s whereabouts must be acted upon with urgency.
The judge's remarks followed a submission by Indira’s lawyer, Harsimran Kaur, who highlighted a glaring delay in police action. According to Harsimran, the police received critical information from the Employees Provident Fund (EPF) on April 24, 2026, regarding the employers of Muhammad Riduan and his current wife, believed to be based in Kelantan.
Despite this lead, the police only moved to investigate on June 15—nearly two months later. Upon arriving at the address linked to the wife’s employer, officers found the premises locked and unoccupied.
“I am not questioning the efforts of the police, but I do not understand the delay. Why was action not taken immediately?” Harsimran asked. She further noted that while the EPF provided the employers' addresses, it failed to specify when the relevant withdrawals had been made.
Norsharidah subsequently reminded investigating officer Assistant Superintendent Yap Siew Cheng of the Perak police contingent’s Criminal Investigation Department, and Federal Counsel Safiyyah Omar, that time is of the essence.
“Once you receive the information, action must be taken quickly. Sometimes, if you wait too long, the person may no longer be there,” the judge said. “In this case, two months later, you were informed that the person had disappeared and was no longer at the location. You need to be more proactive.”
While the judge maintained that she does not need to "teach the police how to make an arrest," she said that the hunt for Riduan is a strategic matter that requires a more aggressive approach.

Title: Malaysia climbs to 12th spot in global peace rankings, among Asia’s safest nations
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 25 — Malaysia has strengthened its position as one of the safest countries in the world, ranking 12th out of 163 nations in the Global Peace Index (GPI) 2026, up one place from 13th position in 2025.
Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the achievement places Malaysia amongst the safest countries in the Asia region alongside Singapore and Japan.
He said the achievement reflects the continuous efforts of the Madani Government in ensuring that security, safety and stability remain the nation’s priorities.
“This recognition is also a result of the commitment of all security personnel, enforcement agencies and all parties who have never compromised in maintaining the nation’s security and public peace,” he said in a statement on Facebook today.
He said the Home Ministry would continue to strengthen efforts to enhance public safety, strengthen law enforcement, tighten control of national borders, combat crime and ensure public peace is always preserved.
Saifuddin said security is not merely an achievement but rather the foundation for public welfare, investor confidence and the nation’s economic growth.
He said efforts to maintain Malaysia as a safe, secure and stable country would continue to be strengthened for the benefit of all Malaysians.
“Congratulations and appreciation to all members of the security forces, enforcement agencies and all Malaysians who have played a role in maintaining the security we enjoy today,” he said.
Earlier, the GPI placed Singapore at eighth position globally, followed by Japan at 10th place and Malaysia at 12th, making the three countries amongst the safest in the Asia region.

Title: Sedition charge for Chegubard over social media post on Negeri Sembilan royalty
Article:
SEREMBAN, June 25 — Badrul Hisham Shaharin, better known as Chegubard, pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to a charge of publishing seditious material against the Negeri Sembilan Royal Institution via his Facebook account last month.
The 48-year-old political activist entered the plea before Judge Datin Surita Budin after the charge was read out to him.
According to the charge, he is alleged to have published seditious content on Facebook under the account Che GuBard on May 26 at approximately 8.30 pm in Kuala Pilah.
He was charged under Section 4(1) of the Sedition Act 1948, which carries a maximum fine of RM5,000 or imprisonment of up to three years, or both, upon conviction.
Deputy public prosecutor Datuk Ahmad Sazali Omar proposed bail at RM10,000 and applied for the court to issue a gag order prohibiting Badrul Hisham from making any statements concerning the Negeri Sembilan Royal Institution.
However, Badrul Hisham’s lawyer, Muhammad Rafique Rashid Ali, applied for bail to be reduced to RM2,500 and objected to the gag order sought against his client.
The court subsequently set bail at RM5,000 with one surety, along with an additional condition that the accused must report to the nearest police station on a monthly basis.
The court also dismissed the application for the gag order, while setting Aug 3 as the next case mention date. — Bernama

Title: Malaysia developing national water reclamation policy to power data centre growth, says Fadillah
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 25 — The government is undertaking a comprehensive study to establish policy and infrastructure foundations for a national water reclamation policy to support the development of data centres, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof.
He said the government plans to establish the initiative and will subsequently develop new policies and strategies for the future.
“This approach will involve engaging all stakeholders, including those from the ministry, the federal government, state governments, and various non-governmental organisations, among others.
“They will be involved later on,” Fadillah told reporters after delivering his opening keynote address at the 5th World Digital Economy & Technology Summit 2026, here today.
Fadillah, who is also Energy Transition and Water Transformation Minister, said Malaysia currently produces 48.5 million litres per day of reclaimed water and is targeting 118 million litres per day by 2030 for industrial use.
Meanwhile, he said the PETRA Strategic Plan 2026-2030 translates that vision into implementation through 119 programmes and 133 measurable targets, with grid modernisation, digitalisation and demand-side management at its core.
On the supply side, Fadillah said Malaysia has surpassed 13.3 gigawatts of installed renewable energy capacity as of 2025, with solar energy recording the fastest growth.
The government is also developing a 1,000-megawatt solar project to supply clean energy to the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone, directly supporting the digital infrastructure ecosystem emerging within that corridor.
“Taken together, these are not isolated initiatives. They form an integrated national response to a challenge that sits at the intersection of energy, water and digital infrastructure,” he added. — Bernama

Title: Onn Hafiz hits back at Puad Zarkashi, says royal consent is constitutional process not royal instruction
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, June 25 — Johor Umno Liaison Committee chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has strongly denied allegations by former Umno Supreme Council member Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi that he had said the palace had ‘ordered’ the dissolution of the Johor legislative assembly.
The Menteri Besar said the dissolution was carried out following the consent of Johor Regent Tunku Mahkota Ismail, in accordance with Article 23, Second Part of the Laws of the State of Johor.
He clarified that the Menteri Besar cannot dissolve the state legislative assembly at will, as the process must strictly follow the provisions set out in the Laws of the State of Johor and only takes effect after royal consent is granted.
“I had an audience and presented the matter. After receiving consent, I announced the dissolution of the state legislative assembly to the people.”
“Obtaining royal consent is a constitutional process and in no way implies receiving instructions or political interference,” he said in a statement today.
He described any attempt to misrepresent the legitimate constitutional process as highly irresponsible, as it could create the perception that the royal institution had interfered in Johor Umno’s political decisions.
Onn Hafiz also said that while Mohd Puad has the right to leave Umno and hold differing views from the party leadership, the statement in question was very serious.
He said such remarks could touch on 3R sensitivities, particularly those involving the royal institution, and could also affect public harmony and order.
Following this development, he said Johor Umno will lodge a police report to enable investigations and further action by the authorities.
He also urged all parties to respect the royal institution, adhere to the constitution, and refrain from exploiting the institution in political disputes.
Earlier, Mohd Puad announced that he had resigned from the party with immediate effect. — Bernama

Title: Umno sec-gen alleges Puad Zarkashi quit because his son was not picked for Johor election
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 25 — Umno secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki has responded to the resignation of Datuk Mohd Puad Zarkashi, claiming the decision was motivated by a disagreement over candidate selection for the Rengit state seat.
Asyraf’s remarks follow Puad’s announcement earlier today that he was leaving the party effective immediately. Puad, the former Johor State Assembly Speaker, had cited alleged external interference in the dissolution of the Johor State Legislative Assembly on June 1 as his primary reason for exiting.
In a statement today, Asyraf characterised Puad’s claims of interference as "slanderous" and asserted that the veteran leader had previously pressured the party to field his son as a candidate.
“He himself had written to me at length, threatening to leave the party and to attack the party if the Umno Supreme Council did not consider fielding his son as a candidate in the Rengit state constituency,” Asyraf said.
While acknowledging the potential of Puad’s son as a young leader, Asyraf maintained that the party must weigh various factors when selecting candidates for any election.
He said that it was "improper" for a senior leader to make his son’s candidacy a condition for remaining with the party.
“Umno is not a party of hereditary succession that must prioritise family and relatives to become leaders. The party’s struggle is far greater than personal interests, family demands, or individual disappointment,” he added.
Asyraf further alleged that this was not the first instance where Puad had threatened to leave the party over personal grievances.
He claimed that during Datuk Seri Najib Razak’s tenure as party president, Puad had made similar threats when he was not re-nominated as the member of parliament for Batu Pahat.
Expressing disappointment at the timing of the resignation ahead of the Johor state election, Asyraf noted that the party had provided numerous opportunities and positions to Puad throughout his career.
He cited the Malay proverb “air susu dibalas air tuba” to describe the situation, suggesting that the party’s past support was being met with hostility.
The secretary-general then said Umno would continue its struggle for "race, religion, and homeland" and would not be swayed by individual pressure.
Today’s development takes place just before nomination day this Saturday for the July 11 Johor state election.

Title: Malaysia acquires French CAESAR artillery systems, eyes homegrown weapons for ANKA-S fleet, says Khaled
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 25 — Malaysia has formally procured CAESAR 155mm self-propelled howitzers (SPH) from France, Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin confirmed today.
“I can confirm that we have agreed on that procurement. That will be another asset added to our arsenal,” he told reporters after launching the National Defence Strategic Plan (PSPN) and the National Defence Capacity Action Plan at Royale Chulan Kuala Lumpur today.
However, Khaled said details on the delivery timeline would be announced later and stressed that the procurement process would be carried out transparently.
“We have nothing to hide,” he added.
The Finance Ministry had reportedly given the green light for the procurement in 2024, but the Defence Ministry was asked to renegotiate the price of the artillery system before finalising the deal.
The Defence Ministry also reportedly allocated RM50 million for the first phase of the acquisition in November 2025 and said the process would be finalised this year.
Separately, Khaled defended Malaysia’s decision to acquire three ANKA-S unmanned aircraft systems from Turkey without offensive weaponry, citing operational requirements and cost-effectiveness.
“The purpose of ANKA-S is for surveillance and to gather intelligence.
“So, if it detects the presence of suspicious assets in our territories, we can deploy our other existing assets to neutralise the threats.
“The weapons for ANKA-S cost almost RM300 million. So, we will fit it with weapons only if we want to deploy it for offensive missions,” he said.
Khaled said Malaysia plans to acquire nine ANKA-S units in phases, with the first batch of three already deployed at the Labuan Air Base.
He said the agreement with Turkey also includes a technology transfer component that would enable Malaysia to develop its own weapon systems for the unmanned aircraft.
“So, when we develop our own weapons, then we will fit them into it (ANKA-S),” he added.

Title: MyKad-linked diesel subsidy reforms could save Putrajaya up to RM2b annually, says Amir Hamzah
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 25 — Putrajaya’s pivot towards using the MyKad for subsidised diesel and uniformed pricing could save the government up to RM2 billion a year, Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan told Parliament this morning.
The system change was part of reforms the Anwar government announced recently as it battles massive diesel smuggling in the country’s east coast states, where the price is slightly cheaper than mainland Malaysia.
Subsidised diesel leakage persisted despite efforts to curb smuggling. The minister revealed the amount of diesel sold at petrol stations in Sabah and Sarawak in March and April 2026 reached 200 million litres per month, estimated to be twice as high as the expected level based on the number of registered diesel vehicles.
“The reform gives the government the opportunity to allocate a portion of these savings to lower the price of subsidised diesel to RM2.10, down from RM2.15 per litre,” he said during Question Time.
“This means that this reform allows Malaysians to enjoy the privilege of citizenship by obtaining diesel at a lower price.”
Before the switch to identification-based distribution, the government adopted a cash transfer system for subsidised diesel for West Malaysia, while Sabah and Sarawak received direct subsidies at the pump.
The subsidised rate of diesel in the two states was RM2.15 per litre. Putrajaya said this led to leakages resulting from smuggling and abuse by non-citizens and industries.
Amir said the switch to the MyKad system for diesel came on the back of BUDI95’s major success of verifying fuel subsidy eligibility using the chip-based identification card.
Based on BUDI95 usage data between October 2025 and May 2026, less than 1 per cent of BUDI95 users consistently consumed more than 200 litres of RON95.
Referring to diesel consumption data from the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) statistics, over 80 per cent of users recorded usage below the 200-litre per month threshold, with an average consumption of around 140 litres per month.
“Nevertheless, taking into account that the majority of the rakyat’s diesel vehicles are jeeps or pickup trucks...the government is providing an additional BUDI MADANI eligibility of another 100 litres that can be applied for,” Amir said, adding this segment serves as the primary transport for goods by small traders and passenger vehicles in the rural interiors.
DOSM data indicates that 95 per cent of diesel users consume less than 300 litres per month, he added, making total eligibility of 300 litres per month sufficient for the vast majority.

Title: Indira Gandhi fails in second bid to cite IGP for contempt over daughter recovery case
Article:
IPOH, June 25 — The High Court here today dismissed M. Indira Gandhi’s application for leave to initiate contempt of court proceedings against the Inspector-General of Police (IGP).
Justice Datuk Norsharidah Awang made the decision after hearing submissions from both parties and dismissed the application with no order as to costs.
In delivering her decision, Norsharidah said evidence before the court showed that investigations had commenced following the receipt of information, and that the investigative process was still ongoing.
“Under these circumstances, the court is unable to conclude that the proposed contemnors have failed or refused to comply with the court’s directions, as the very investigation ordered by the court has yet to be completed,” she said.
Norsharidah said the court could not make a finding of non-compliance before the investigative process had been allowed to run its full course.
“To do so would be premature and would amount to pre-judging matters that remain the subject of an active inquiry,” she said.
She added that the court was of the view that the application was filed before sufficient time had elapsed for authorities to investigate the newly surfaced information and take any action that might be warranted.
“As such, the court finds no prima facie evidence of deliberate disobedience,” she said.
Norsharidah also held that the evidence relied upon by the applicant, including the alleged utilisation of Sara 100 and Budi 95 by K. Pathmanathan, was insufficient to establish a prima facie case warranting the grant of leave to commence committal proceedings against the proposed contemnors.
“Based on the reasons stated above, the applicant has failed to disclose a prima facie case of contempt, and the application for leave to issue committal proceedings against the proposed contemnors is therefore dismissed, with no order as to costs.
“Notwithstanding the dismissal of the present application, the proposed contemnors remain under a continuing obligation to take all reasonable and proactive measures to execute the outstanding warrant of committal and to continue reporting to the court in accordance with the existing orders of the court,” she said.
This is the second time Indira’s attempt to cite the IGP for contempt of court has been rejected.
The first application was filed on Oct 28, 2020, naming the IGP, the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM), the Home Ministry and the government as the first to fourth defendants. The High Court dismissed the application on Aug 2, 2022.
The second application was filed on Nov 17 last year and was heard by the High Court on Feb 27 this year.
Indira’s application centred on the police’s alleged continued failure to enforce the High Court’s orders issued on May 30, 2014, specifically to recover her daughter and arrest her former husband, Pathmanathan.

Title: Govt rolls out RM710m package, RM10b guarantees to cushion job losses and support SMEs
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 25 — The government has rolled out a RM710 million labour market resilience package alongside RM10 billion in SME financing guarantees to cushion job losses and support businesses amid global economic uncertainty, including energy price pressures and supply chain disruptions, Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said.
He said the measures, coordinated through the National Economic Action Council (MTEN), form part of an integrated approach to protect workers, sustain businesses and stabilise the labour market.
“The government has focused on ensuring the continuity of operations for micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs) and domestic industries. This includes the RM5 billion Bank Negara Malaysia SME Stabilisation Relief Facility (SME SRF).
“As of June 18, 2026, financing of more than RM700 million had been approved for over 1,000 SMEs,” he told the Dewan Rakyat today.
Akmal was responding to a question from Bachok MP Tuan Mohd Syahir Che Sulaiman on mitigation measures under MTEN to address rising job losses and business downsizing following the energy crisis and global economic uncertainty, as well as the outcomes of those measures.
He said a key pillar of the response was the RM710 million Progressive Acceleration for Capability and Employment (PACE) package, which was designed to strengthen job protection and workforce resilience.
Under PACE, he said more than RM580 million had been channelled through the Social Security Organisation (Socso) to strengthen the Employment Insurance System, while RM100 million had been allocated via HRD Corp for training and job placement programmes supported by MYFutureJobs.
A further RM20 million had been allocated for gig worker training through the Skills Development Fund Corporation (PTPK), alongside RM10 million via TalentCorp to support industrial training for SMEs and start-ups.
Akmal also said Malaysia’s labour market had remained resilient despite external pressures, supported by these interventions.
He noted that the highest number of job losses recorded this year occurred in January, with 10,658 individuals affected.
“The highest number actually occurred in January, with 10,658 individuals losing their jobs.
